methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $17M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Hertha Metals is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $17M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of nine private robotics and manufacturing firms (including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity) and one public comparable (277810.KQ).

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method. Using total capital raised ($17M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, this method implies a valuation of $34M. A peer-median approach could not be reliably applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the robotics and manufacturing sector meeting minimum statistical threshold (fewer than three datapoints after filtering by stage and valuation tier).

The triangulated valuation range is $29M to $39M, with a central estimate of $34M. This range reflects the constraints of single-method derivation. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier to Hertha Metals' specific growth trajectory and capital intensity, the relevance of the broader sector peer set given the limited same-stage comparable data, and market conditions in robotics and manufacturing financing. A second valuation method—whether through expanded peer analysis, discounted cash flow, or transaction precedent—would substantially improve conviction in this range.
